entry.title: Portuguese National Budget for 2025- Payroll Legal Changes
entry.content:
Happy New Year 2025 to you All.

As we embrace this festive season, we wish you a prosperous New Year in 2025.

It's a time for celebration and joy, and we want to take this opportunity to share some important updates regarding the upcoming State Budget for 2025, which will bring changes to the salary system.

Before diving into the details, here's what you need to know first:

We would like to share with you the upcoming changes regarding the State Budget for 2025, which will impact the salary system. But first, let us identify what is most important:

  • Minimum Support Package Level for implementing the new updates of the 2025 State Budget:

Release

Support Package

S4HCM 101

HR SP 00

S4HCM 100

HR SP 04

SAP_HR 6.00

HR SP M1

SAP_HR 6.04

HR SP I7

SAP_HR 6.08

HR SP B5

As you all know, it is necessary to have these Support Packages at a minimum in order to install the notes we plan to publish in January with the solutions.

We are focused on delivering a solution for the following scenarios:

  • Exempt Lunch Allowance amount for 2025 (Limite de isenção Subsídio de Refeição em senhas para 2025)
  • Changes of Index dos Socials Support (IAS) constant for 2025 (alteração do Indexante dos Apoios Sociais 2025)
  • Minimun Wage for Portugal (mainland) and Azores Islands 2025 (Salário Minimo do Continente e Açores 2025)
  • Minimum Wage for Madeira Islands 2025
  • IRS Incentive to Hire Young People 2025 (Incentivo de IRS Jovens 2025)
  • Overtime for residents – New tax autonomous rate for all the number of hours (novo cálculo de de taxa de IRS Trabalho Suplementar 2025) 
  • Overtime for non residents – New number of hours of tax exempt, from limit of 50 hours to the limit of 100 hours of overtime (Novo cálculo de limite de horas de isenção de IRS Trabalho Suplementar não residentes) 

Additional scenarios may arise following the publication of the State Budget Law. This planning summary is being made based on the text of the proposed State Budget Law for 2025.

We would thus like to highlight the expected changes for the Young People IRS incentive:

  • Consideration of 10 years of applicability instead of 5 years
  • The monthly benefit limit will be the same for all years (55xIAS/14)
  • We present the planned table for this benefit:

Year

Benefit (%)

Monthly Limit Formula

Monthly exemption limit amount

1

100

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

2

75

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

3

75

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

4

75

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

5

50

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

6

50

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

7

50

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

8

25

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

9

25

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

10

25

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

Regarding the taxation (tax retention) of overtime, according to the proposed law, paragraph 8 of Article 99-C of the CIRS will be amended as follows (in bold):

“8- When remuneration for overtime work is paid, the withholding tax rate to be applied is 50% of the rate applicable to the dependent work monthly remuneration for the month in which the overtime is paid or made available.”

Paragraph 10 of the same article will be repealed :

“10 - The autonomous withholding tax rate applied to remuneration for overtime work, as provided in paragraphs 5 and 8, is reduced by 50% from the 101st hour onward, inclusive.”

These changes are expected to simplify the IRS calculations for overtime work that were introduced in 2023, making the process more streamlined and efficient.

Regarding Overtime for non residents – New number of hours of tax exempt (from limit of 50 hours to the limit of 100 hours of overtime) with the maximum exempt amount that was already set of RMMG (€870,00).

We hope you find these updates helpful as we move into the new year. Thank you for your attention, and here's to a remarkable 2025!

entry.title: Counter Routing more than 99 in Manage Routings / Create Routing app in S/4HANA Cloud ?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Counter Routing more than 99 in Manage Routings / Cre...
entry.content:
The field Group counter (PLKO-PLNAL) is a char 2 field so can only contain a numeric value up to 99. However, the two-digit field is alpha-numeric so there is larger range available.

Enter an alpha-numeric value such as "N1" for more than 99 entries

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content:
Regarding the paging issue:

How to implement paging for a custom entity is described in https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/requesting-and-setting-data-or-count-in-unmanaged-query?version=Cloud&locale=en-US
There the following note is given: "If data is requested, the method set_data must be called. If the total number of records is requested, the method set_total_number_of_records must be called. Otherwise there will be an error during runtime. The io_response->set_data( ) method is only allowed to fill elements listed by io_request->get_requested_elements( ). System may react with a failure or a short dump if these rules aren't followed. In UI scenarios with Fiori Elements the total number of records is always requested by the UI."

Regarding the available CDS views:

The field type "Code List based on CDS View" and also the restrictions regarding available CDS views is explained here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/acb2907f362944a5a9e1b5eb86e769dc.html

One can sum up the restrictions as follows: "You can either select a CDS-based view (Custom CDS View/SAP delivered CDS view released according to the C1 contract), or you can select a custom entity."

entry.title: Amortization Model in SAC
entry.content:
I am working on an amortization model for my project in SAC, and I am facing the following issue:

I have an Excel dataset with the following information:

  • Asset type (e.g., A, B, C, etc.)
  • Useful life (in months)
  • Acquisition value
  • Acquisition date
  • Start of payment date

What I would like to implement in SAC is:

  1. A table showing:
    • The start of payment date.
    • The monthly payments over the asset's useful life.
  2. For each row in the table, the monthly payment value would be calculated as:
    Acquisition value / Useful life.

Practical example:

  • Asset type: A
  • Useful life: 6 months
  • Acquisition value: 5000
  • Acquisition date: 2024-01 (January 2024)
  • Start of payment date: 2024-02 (February 2024)

In the table, the payments would appear as follows:

  • February 2024: 833.33
  • March 2024: 833.33
  • April 2024: 833.33
  • May 2024: 833.33
  • June 2024: 833.33
  • July 2024: 833.33

In other words, the total acquisition value is divided by the useful life and proportionally distributed across the corresponding months. I need help understanding how to implement this model in SAC so that the table is generated correctly.

entry.title: Maximizing BTP Job Scheduling Service with SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model
entry.content:
Hi SAP PAPM UM Community, 

Before I start my blogpost, I would like to appreciate your commitment in learning and understanding SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud, Universal Model (SAP PAPM Cloud UM). With such interest, our team is getting fueled with writing more and more blogposts that hopefully can help you in your journey with SAP PAPM Cloud UM.

ℹ️ INFORMATION
If you are new to the blogpost series, please visit this site for more interesting blogposts 

Today as my final entry for the year 2024, I would like to share with you a trick on how you can leverage some actions such as Scheduled Activity Execution with the help of SAP Job Scheduling Service (SAP JSS). With this technique you can then schedule your Activity execution for a one-time purpose or if need be periodically. 

Just to organize our thoughts, let us discuss this topic into phases by setting up

Phase I. SAP BTP: Application and Instance Subscriptions
Phase II. SAP BTP: Creation of BTP Customized Application
Phase III. SAP PAPM Cloud UM: Collect Activity API (Action URL)
Phase IV. SAP JSS: Job Creation
Phase V. SAP JSS: Job Scheduling
Phase VI. SAP PAPM Cloud UM & SAP JSS: Run validation 

 

⚠️ WARNING
Assumption is you already have an active SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model Application with Environment and Activities. 

Let us proceed.

 

Phase I - SAP BTP: Application and Instance Subscriptions

1) Subaccount Entitlement

In case you are only subscribed to SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model Application and not to the other services needed then you need to do entitlement for below services. 

ServicePurpose
SAP PAPM Cloud UM
Plan: Default Service Instance

This will be used to bind the custom application that we are to create later in Phase II to SAP PAPM Cloud UM.

⚠️ SAP Job Scheduling Service is not allowing credential entry, so we will use a BTP created application to bridge SAP JSS and SAP PAPM Cloud UM. You will know more about this in Phase 2.

 

Cloud Foundry Runtime
Plan: Memory

This is necessary in order for us to deploy or create a custom application in our subaccount. 

Authorization and Trust Management Service
Plan: Application 

This is a prerequisite of Job Scheduler which helps in binding the custom application that we are to create later in Phase II to SAP Job Scheduling Service

Job Scheduling Service
Plan: Standard

Will be used to schedule the activity run either as one time execution or periodic. 

 

2) Cloud Foundry Activation

If your subaccount is on AWS EU10, ensure to activate cloud foundry in landscape cf-eu10-004. Custom Application creation (for our Phase II) in cf-eu10 landscape is not permitted by BTP due to memory restriction. 

 

3) Cloud Foundry Space Creation

Create a space where the services and the custom application will be created. 

 

4) SAP PaPM Cloud Service Instance and Service Key Creation

To create SAP PaPM Cloud Service Instance follow the official help documentation .
Ensure to also use the scope provided by the guide in order to get proper access to SAP PaPM Cloud Applications. 

 

5) Job Scheduling Service Instance Creation

Follow steps 1-5 of the following official help guide to create a job scheduling service instance. Ignore for now step 6, that will be performed in the next phase. 

⚠️ WARNING
It is important to remember the Job Scheduling Service Instance Name -- in this case I named it jss. This instance name is to be used as part of the XSUAA's scope definition. 

 

6) XSUAA Service Creation with SAP JSS Scopes

As a security prerequisite of Job Scheduling Service, Standard Plan; an Authorization and Trust Management Service must be created with the SAP JSS scopes. Inspired by the scope provided by the SAP JSS official documentation, we created below that you can also copy upon XSUAA instance creation. 

 

{
  "xsappname": "jba-application",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Access",
      "grant-as-authority-to-apps": ["$XSSERVICENAME(<jobscheduler instance name>)"]
    }
  ],
  "authorities": ["$XSAPPNAME.Access"],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "Access",
      "scope-references": ["$XSAPPNAME.Access"]
    }
  ]
}

 

 

Now that we have all the services necessary, let us proceed with Phase II and create our Application. 

 

Phase II - SAP BTP: Creation of Customized Application

Since SAP Job Scheduling Service is not allowing a direct ClientID, ClientSecret (or credential) input, we will create an application that will serve as a proxy to create token needed by the jobscheduler to avoid authentication issue upon calling SAP PAPM Cloud UM Application or Activity like below. 

With the help of a developer teammate, we have created below SAMPLE custom files that you may also use as inspiration in creating your custom proxy application (of course you are free to adjust it and make it more sophisticated)

1) package.json

 

{
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/xsenv": "^5.4.0",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.2.7",
    "axios": "^1.7.9",
    "express": "^4.21.2",
    "http-proxy-middleware": "^3.0.3",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18",
    "npm": "^9"
  }
}

 

 

2) manifest.yml

change line

  • 3 with the application name that you want to use.
  • 7 with your application route
  • 10 with your xsuaa service name
  • 11 with your papm service name
  • 13 & 14 with your technical user's credentials

 

---
applications:
- name: jobscheduler-papm-proxy2
  buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  command: node index.js
  routes:
    - route: jobscheduler-papm-proxy2.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com
  services:
    #servicenames of your xsuaa and papm service
    - xsuaaservice 
    - papmservice
  env:
    BTP_USERNAME: "<change with your technical id>"
    BTP_PASSWORD: "<password of your techincal id>"

 

 

3) index.js

change line

  • 11 with label: "<xsuaa service name>"
  • 87 with label: "<papm service name>"

 

const express = require("express");
const axios = require("axios");
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
const { stringify } = require("querystring");
const { serviceCredentials } = require("@sap/xsenv");
const { createSecurityContext, XsuaaService } = require("@sap/xssec");
const { createProxyMiddleware, errorResponsePlugin } = require("http-proxy-middleware");

const app = express();

const xsuaaService = serviceCredentials({ label: "xsuaaservice" });
const authService = new XsuaaService(xsuaaService);
const authMiddleware = async (req, res, next) => {
  try {
    req.securityContext = await createSecurityContext(authService, { req });
    return next();
  } catch (e) {
    return res.status(401).json(e);
  }
};

app.get("/", (req, res) => res.send("OK"));

app.use(authMiddleware);
app.get("/authentication", (req, res) =>
  res.send(req.securityContext.token.userId)
);

const scopeMiddleware = (req, res, next) => {
  if (req.securityContext.checkLocalScope("Access")) {
    return next();
  } else {
    res.sendStatus(403);
  }
};

app.use(scopeMiddleware);
app.get("/authorization", (req, res) =>
  res.json(req.securityContext.token.scopes)
);

class RequestService {
  constructor(credentials) {
    this.credentials = credentials;
    this.cache = null;
  }
  async getOAuthToken(grant_type = "client_credentials", parameters = {}) {
    if (this.isValidToken()) return this.getCache();
    const { clientid, clientsecret, url } = this.credentials.uaa;
    const data = stringify(parameters);
    try {
      const response = await axios.post(
        `${url}/oauth/token?grant_type=${grant_type}`,
        data,
        {
          headers: data ? { 'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' } : {},
          auth: {
            username: clientid,
            password: clientsecret,
          },
        }
      );
      this.setCache(response.data);
      return response.data;
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(err);
    }
  }
  isValidToken() {
    const data = this.getCache();
    if (!data) return false;
    const now = Date.now();
    const decoded = jwt.decode(data.access_token);
    if (!decoded) return false;
    const remainingTime = (Number(decoded.exp) * 1000 - now) / (1000 * 60);
    if (remainingTime < 30) return false;
    return true;
  }
  setCache(value) {
    this.cache = value;
  }
  getCache() {
    return this.cache;
  }
}

const papmService = serviceCredentials({ label: "papmservice" });
const username = process.env.BTP_USERNAME;
const password = process.env.BTP_PASSWORD;
const grant_type = username && password ? "password" : "client_credentials";
const parameters = username && password ? { username, password } : {};
const requestService = new RequestService(papmService);

const proxyMiddleware = createProxyMiddleware({
  target: `https://${papmService.url}`,
  changeOrigin: true,
  pathRewrite: async (path, req) => {
    const token = await requestService.getOAuthToken(grant_type, parameters);
    req.headers.authorization = `Bearer ${token.access_token}`;
    return path;
  },
  on: {
    proxyReq: (proxyReq, req) => {
      const { method, path, headers = proxyReq.getHeaders() } = proxyReq;
      console.log(`Proxy sending request: ${method} ${path}; ${Object.entries(headers).map(([k, v]) => `${k}: ${v}`).join("; ")}`);
    },
    proxyRes: (proxyRes, req) => {
      const { statusCode, statusMessage, req: { _header } } = proxyRes;
      console.log(`Proxy receiving response: ${_header.trim().split(/\r\n/).join("; ")} - ${statusCode} ${statusMessage}`);
    },
    error: (error, req, res, target) => {
      console.log(`Proxy error: ${error.toString()}, on target: ${target && target.href}`);
    },
  },
  plugins: [errorResponsePlugin],
});
app.use(proxyMiddleware);

const port = process.env.PORT ?? 3000;
app.listen(port);

 

 

4) Create a folder with the files and a zip version

 

5) Deploy the Custom Application (proxy)

 

6) Check the Application's Service Binding

It is important to see om the Service Bindings section "automatically" both PaPM and XSUAA services

 

7) Ensure to assign the technical user with proper role (collections)

It is important to assign the technical user (declared in the manifest.yml) with proper SAP PAPM Cloud UM roles as well as the "Access"  role generated by the deployed custom proxy application.

 

Now that we are all set with Phases I & II, I will now teach you how to collect or take note of the SAP PAPM Cloud UM API URL to serve as your action URL when setting up a SAP JSS job. 

 

Phase III - SAP PAPM Cloud UM: Collect activity API (Action URL)

Assuming that you already have a PAPM Cloud UM Activity that is being wanted to be scheduled for execution via SAP Job Scheduling Service. It is then very important to get the action URL or Activity API URL that can be fed to SAP JSS. The format would be

<BTP Proxy Application Routes>/ace/<$batch's request url>/<$batch's payload>
Something like

https://jobscheduler-papm-proxy.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ace/sap/ace/ProcessService/Activities(ID='422e06bf-abc2-46a2-ac32-db7cefbf0fc0',IsActiveEntity=true)/sap.ace.ProcessService.runActivity
 

ℹ️INFORMATION
You need to enable the Developer Tools on your browser to be guided with the accurate $batch request URL and payload

 

The original $batch information could be collected upon choosing or pressing an action such as activity "Run". Make sure that you change the first few parts of the request URL with the proxy application route we created in Phase II.  

 

Phase IV - SAP JSS: Job Creation

Now that we have all the information we need, we are then ready to create a job in SAP Job Scheduling Service.

ℹ️ INFORMATION
SAP Job Scheduling Service uses UTC (that is -1 CET Time). 

 

Phase V - SAP JSS: Job Scheduling

Using the official guide, you will see that there are atleast 3 types of schedule Formats in SAP JSS such as Cron, Date/Time, and Human-Readable Date Formats. I am personally more comfortable with cron hence will be giving my example in cron form. Feel free to check which option(s) is more comfortable for you.

 

ℹ️ INFORMATION
SAP Job Scheduling Service uses UTC (that is -1 CET Time). Also it is important to know that the {} in the Data (JSON) is important for the scheduler to execute SAP PAPM UM Activities using the Custom Proxy Application I provided in Phase II.  

1) One-Time Run

 

2) Scheduled to run every 5 minutes (via cron)

The job that I will schedule means that it will run every 5minutes "From" the time set until the time "To". If you are interested to know more how to handle cron format, visit the official documentation.

I will use * * * * * */5 0
>> this means, for any year, month, day, day of the week, hour, it will run every 5minutes at exactly 0 second. 

 

Now that we have scheduled the run, let us validate that the run was successful by checking both SAP PAPM UM (event logs) and SAP JSS (Run logs)

 

Phase VI - SAP PAPM Cloud UM & SAP JSS: Run Validation

1) SAP JSS

You can see in the Run Log that the job is executing every 5 minutes successfully from 16:05 UTC. 

 

2) SAP PaPM Cloud UM

You can see in SAP PAPM Cloud UM's Event Logs that the runs were also successful at exactly 17:05 - onwards every 5 minutes of my local time, CET Time. 

 

Success!!! ☁️ ⏰ 🔄

Alright, that is all for now! I hope this excites you and helps you moving forward with your job scheduling agenda with SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model.

Have a pleasant year end, and see you next year in our 2025 blogpost entries!

entry.title: Re: Unable to Update manually added Standard Fields to St...
entry.content:
Hello @Sanket001,

to add a standard field on the standard API just in output (GET) you can create an Extension view (you can find a sample on the prevoius post) just adding the field you need. In my case this worked properly.
To update a stardard field using API (POST/PATCH/PUT), my approach was to create a custom field, copy of the standard field, using SAP Fiori Extensibility ->Custom Fields and using an exit to move the value of the custom field on the standard field.
If you need more information, don't hesitate to contact me 😉

Best regards,
F.

entry.title: Re: Running Year to Date (YTD) on fiscal periods with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@MariannevL 

1) Are you really sure that the execution plan do not "fallback to older engines" ?

from what I see HEX uses way less memory than JOIN/OLAP/CALC engines.

2) Non equi join are killer to HANA. With HEX they introduce specific phisical operators to deal with non equi join ("range joins"), while the older engines can not do this kind of join efficiently => is not that HEX sucks in equi-join, rather than HEX works better in non-equi-join in comparison to the older engines. from what I see HEX suffer a lot from cast and trim on join column, more than the previous engines, but in general it works better il 99% of the time.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Purchase Order Create while transfer Shipment Cost.

entry.title: Re: Running QTD, YTD in SAP Datasphere using SQL View
entry.content:
Did the model even suggest that when during a fiscal period there are no posting on one particular account, with the window function the cumulated value for that account do not appear in that specific period? (=> wrong totals)

Since LLM are trained "generally", while the query optimization depends heavily on the specific DBMS it is executed, are you really sure that the query given is the optimal for HANA that is an in memory column store database ? (did you test it with PlanViz/ SQL Analyzer? the last time I check performances were the same)

"using window functions is more efficient than using subqueries or self-joins":

- is totally not trivial as untill like yesterday window function were executed in ROW engine leading to OOM issues due to row format Itab.

- range joins operators were added to HEX to improve non equi joins performances.

entry.title: Re: Running a Jupyter notebook in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: Struggled with this for a long time, using BAS found that PIP INSTALL puts the packages in a different place than Jupyter notebook is looking for them. Had to add destination using SYS.PATH.APPEND and it was necessary to user IMPORT SYS first. Had to find the path first using "CD /HOME/" from the terminal then navigating down, /USER/.ASDF-INST/INSTALLS/PYTHON/3.11.2/LIB/PYTHON3.11/SITE-PACKAGES. Would be interested to know if there's a better way to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service - Part 1
entry.content:
I found the solution, I had to add the destination at subaccount level and not at instance level:

subaccount:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
            HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
            Name: RoyCustDeclaration-srv-api
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
          existing_destinations_policy: update

thank you also for the hint about the "csrfProtection" issue.

Ciao ciao!

 

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content: My whole family (minus one nephew) was together for Christmas Eve and Christmas Day this year. This is the spread that my mom put together for all of us (14 people). My mom is an amazing cook!! Christmas meal with vegetable tray, meat and cheese tray, various fruits, lettuce, refrigerator pickles, tomatoes,ham, turkey, stuffing, sweet potatoes (2 ways), deviled eggs, creamed corn, green beans, au gratin potatoes, gravy, green olives and dinner rolls.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
@Janet_Salmon thank you for your response.  I had another followup to @PreethaB but I am not sure if she received it.

Since UPA is supported only for 5 countries, how would our Brazil subsidiary be handled?  Would the USA use UPA and Brazil use the old currency/valuation profile approach?

Or does the system stop you from creating any Brazil-based postings at all?

Thank you!

entry.title: MATDOC IS getting in message for NRGP =1 BUT NOT FOR NRGP =2
entry.content:
MODULE USER_COMMAND INPUT.

GV_UCOMM = SY-UCOMM.
DATA : LV_REQ TYPE ZREQ.
DATA : LV_IREQ TYPE ZIREQ.
DATA : LV_INTER(10) TYPE N."zinter.
DATA : GJAHR(8) TYPE C.
DATA: LV_ZTAG1 TYPE T052-ZTAG1,
LV_ZTERM TYPE LFM1-ZTERM.


CLEAR: wa_gmitem, wa_gmhead,matdoc, it_gmitem.

IF GV_UCOMM = '&SAVE'.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB
IF CUST EQ 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-KUNNR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
ENDIF.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB

IF ( "zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-werks IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lgort IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lifnr IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-nrgp IS INITIAL ).

MESSAGE: 'Please enter No of Packages, Plant, Stor. Loc., Vendor, RGP'
TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT EQ '00000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT = SY-DATUM.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET EQ '000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET = SY-UZEIT.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH EQ SPACE.
zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH = SY-UNAME.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = SPACE.
SELECT MAX( REQ ) INTO LV_REQ FROM zsc_head_sadm2.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = LV_REQ + 1.
* zsc_head_sadm2-rdate = sy-datum.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '1' AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS INITIAL.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
*************************************************************
LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @DATA(LV_MTART)
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

* IF it_return IS NOT INITIAL.
IF it_return IS INITIAL. """"Added by Akshata
DATA(lv_check) = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

*************************************************************
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' AND lv_check = abap_true.
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'J' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
"""""""Begin of Changes by Eidikos Mahesh K""""""""
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
UPDATE zsc_head_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
CCREADATE = SY-DATUM
CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.
zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ELSEIF ( zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '2'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '3'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '4' )
AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS NOT INITIAL.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'. """"""ADDED BY AKSHATA
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @LV_MTART
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF it_return IS INITIAL.
lv_check = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE """added by akshata z41 to z61
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' and lv_check = abap_true. """added by akshata
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'O' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
zsc_head_sadm2-remarks = zsc_head_sadm2-remarks.
zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg = zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg.

* UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
* WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
* AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.

zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WA_GMHEAD-REF_DOC_NO = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.
WA_LINE-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ NE '0000'.
VAR = WA_LINE-IREQ.
ENDIF.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ EQ '0000'.
VAR = VAR + 1.
WA_LINE-IREQ = VAR.
ENDIF.
WA_LINE-KWERT = WA_LINE-MENGE * WA_LINE-KBETR.
WA_LINE-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
WA_LINE-GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
WA_LINE-REMQTY = WA_LINE-MENGE.

**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

MODIFY it_line FROM wa_line TRANSPORTING kwert
inter
gjahr
remqty.
* MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM WA_LINE.

CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF MATDOC IS NOT INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z41'.

ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSE.
* READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
* IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* SEPARATED BY SPACE.
* MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
* ENDIF.
* ENDIF.

""""""""""""""""""""""Added by Akshata 26.12.2024""""""""""'
ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

""""""""""""ENd of chnages 26.12.2024""""""""""""""""""""""

DATA : MESS TYPE CHAR200.

IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'. "AND matdoc IS NOT INITIAL.
CONCATENATE 'Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-REQ 'AND'
'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
'AND' 'Material Document :' matdoc
'has been saved.'
INTO MESS
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
ENDIF.

entry.title: MATDOC IS GEETING IN MESSAG EFOR NRGP =1 BUT NOT FOR NRGP=2 ..
entry.content:
MODULE USER_COMMAND INPUT.

GV_UCOMM = SY-UCOMM.
DATA : LV_REQ TYPE ZREQ.
DATA : LV_IREQ TYPE ZIREQ.
DATA : LV_INTER(10) TYPE N."zinter.
DATA : GJAHR(8) TYPE C.
DATA: LV_ZTAG1 TYPE T052-ZTAG1,
LV_ZTERM TYPE LFM1-ZTERM.


CLEAR: wa_gmitem, wa_gmhead,matdoc, it_gmitem.

IF GV_UCOMM = '&SAVE'.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB
IF CUST EQ 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-KUNNR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
ENDIF.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB

IF ( "zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-werks IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lgort IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lifnr IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-nrgp IS INITIAL ).

MESSAGE: 'Please enter No of Packages, Plant, Stor. Loc., Vendor, RGP'
TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT EQ '00000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT = SY-DATUM.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET EQ '000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET = SY-UZEIT.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH EQ SPACE.
zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH = SY-UNAME.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = SPACE.
SELECT MAX( REQ ) INTO LV_REQ FROM zsc_head_sadm2.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = LV_REQ + 1.
* zsc_head_sadm2-rdate = sy-datum.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '1' AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS INITIAL.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
*************************************************************
LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @DATA(LV_MTART)
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

* IF it_return IS NOT INITIAL.
IF it_return IS INITIAL. """"Added by Akshata
DATA(lv_check) = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

*************************************************************
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' AND lv_check = abap_true.
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'J' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
"""""""Begin of Changes by Eidikos Mahesh K""""""""
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
UPDATE zsc_head_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
CCREADATE = SY-DATUM
CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.
zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ELSEIF ( zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '2'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '3'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '4' )
AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS NOT INITIAL.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'. """"""ADDED BY AKSHATA
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @LV_MTART
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF it_return IS INITIAL.
lv_check = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE """added by akshata z41 to z61
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' and lv_check = abap_true. """added by akshata
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'O' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
zsc_head_sadm2-remarks = zsc_head_sadm2-remarks.
zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg = zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg.

* UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
* WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
* AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.

zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WA_GMHEAD-REF_DOC_NO = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.
WA_LINE-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ NE '0000'.
VAR = WA_LINE-IREQ.
ENDIF.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ EQ '0000'.
VAR = VAR + 1.
WA_LINE-IREQ = VAR.
ENDIF.
WA_LINE-KWERT = WA_LINE-MENGE * WA_LINE-KBETR.
WA_LINE-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
WA_LINE-GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
WA_LINE-REMQTY = WA_LINE-MENGE.

**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

MODIFY it_line FROM wa_line TRANSPORTING kwert
inter
gjahr
remqty.
* MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM WA_LINE.

CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF MATDOC IS NOT INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z41'.

ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSE.
* READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
* IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* SEPARATED BY SPACE.
* MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
* ENDIF.
* ENDIF.

""""""""""""""""""""""Added by Akshata 26.12.2024""""""""""'
ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

""""""""""""ENd of chnages 26.12.2024""""""""""""""""""""""

DATA : MESS TYPE CHAR200.

IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'. "AND matdoc IS NOT INITIAL.
CONCATENATE 'Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-REQ 'AND'
'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
'AND' 'Material Document :' matdoc
'has been saved.'
INTO MESS
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Why the error loop on a Fiori Elements application when running on SAP Work Zone(Launchpad)
entry.content:
I wrote a CAP application with a Fiori Elements, business as usual.

When writing an event handler, I wanted to reject, return an error, if certain condition is met:

this.on('READ', 'MyEntity', async (req) => {
  // ...
  if (!filter) {
    req.reject(500,'Please select a filter.');
  }
  // ...
  return result;
});

What happens is that, if I run the application standalone, locally, or in BTP's HTML5 Repository, it behaves correctly. When an error happens it returns the error and stops. I fix the input and the app runs fine:

One error

 

However, if I run it on SAP WorkZone, standard edition (former Launchpad), either in BTP or using the cds-launchpad-plugin, it enters in an infinite error loop:

Infinite errors

and the list keeps growing. If I close the error screen it immediately re-opens and keeps outputting more errors. The only way to stop it is to exit the page.

To share some light on what's happening, it keeps sending the same "wrong" request. If I put a breakpoint in my application it will hold and stop the loop. But as soon as I release it, the front-end fails and sends the same request again.

The workaround I had to do was to catch the error and return a valid response:

try {
  //...
} catch (e) {
  // throw e; // doesn't work
  // req.reject(e.message); // doesn't work
  console.error(e.message); // log for information
  // return a valid object, but fill the values with the error information
  return [{
    title: "ERROR",
    description: e.message
  }];
}

 

Let me know if you faced something similar and if you know how to stop this infinite loop.

entry.title: Revenue Increasing the Budget (RIB) in PSM-FM
entry.content:
In public sector organizations, often budgets are made available to the departments when the corresponding revenue is posted. Organizations normally have an approved posted budget, and they would like to supplement it using revenue earned as a source to increase the expenditures budgets.

SAP Funds Management Budget Control System has a functionality to meet this requirement, it is termed as RIB (Revenue Increasing the Budget).

What is RIB:

The term RIB is not known to many of us working in SAP. Even for me it

was a new concept until 2012 when I worked on it for the first time in one of my projects where I was implementing PSM-FM & Grants Management.

From functionality perspective, we know that in PSM-FM we can post consumable budget on the Fund Center and Commitment items, other optional Funds Management Account assignment (FMAA) like Fund, Funded Program etc.  Budget is posted on FMAA and actual and commitments are recorded from various sources like procure to pay cycle and FI expenses posting, HR, EAM etc.

The consumable budget can be changed by using budget supplements postings or transferring budget from other departments. There is another way to increase the budget which is mostly used in the public sector organization which is to increase the consumable budgets using the Revenue recognized as a source. Revenue recognition happens on a budget or posting address i-e the budget FMAA which contains budget or posting address which gets derived during revenue recognition postings. 

How does RIB Work:

The recognized revenue or posted revenue increases the expenditure budget of the receiver FMAA which is maintained in the Derivation strategy for RIB Rule generation FMRBDERIMD - Assign Proposal for RIB Master Data.

For the above to happen, we should declare a derivation strategy for RIB objects as well which specifies the budget address which will contain revenue budget (if needed as per the calculation formula which works with upper and lower limits). Once revenue is posted on FM Account assignments using FI posting or SD billing posting then this derivation strategy for RIB objects & RIB Rules comes into picture which allows us to aggregate revenue from various budget/posting addresses and a percentage of this revenue is used to increase the budget of the expenditure side based on the calculation formula for RIB.

Configuration Steps:

If an organization needs to use RIB functionality, then following must be setup in the IMG PathFunds Management Government >>>BCS >>> Budgeting>>> Control Budgeting Flow>>>Revenues Increasing the Budget:

Define the Use of Budget Memos for RIB:

I am not going to use Budget Memos for RIB hence I choose the option below.

 

 

 

 

 

Define Derivation Strategy for RIB Objects (FMRBDERIRO):

We need to define derivation strategy for RIB objects with source and target address. This will help us determine the RIB objects from the budget address. This is done via FMRBDERIRO - Derive RIB Object from Budget Addr.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The FMAA to which you post revenue using FI/SD postings is assigned to a budget address using this derivation strategy. The RIB object is derived from a revenues budget address using a derivation strategy defined in Customizing. As per above I am deriving Sales Business Development Fund center 9095 & 912001 commitment item for Revenue (30/2) whenever FI posting is done the budget address Fund Center 9090 and Commitment item 912001. 

Maintain Calculation Formulas for RIB:

This is an important step for RIB and without this RIB cannot work. The percentage of revenues field below determines what portion of the revenues in a posting address may have a budget increasing effect on the assigned target budget addresses. 

I created RIB Calculation formula Z001 and stipulated below that 6% of the posted revenue is to increase the budget. If FI invoice gets posted for say 10,000 EUR to revenue posting address, then 600 EUR will be accounted for the budget increase and hence it will flow to the expenditure budget address. The formula is without any upper and lower limits hence does not require revenue budget to be posted first.

 

 

 

 

 

Maintain Derivation Strategy for Rule Generation (FMRBDERIMD):

This very important derivation strategy, derivation strategy for RIB rule contains all information required for increasing the budget of specified expenditure budget addresses from specific revenues. It is assigned to a RIB object and comprises, amongst others, the RIB calculation formula and the receiving budget addressesIn RIB Rule generation FMRBDERIMD, we need to maintain the derivation strategy for proposing values for the automatic generation of rules for Revenues Increasing the Budget.

We must define the source fields by assigning the following values to a given RIB object.

  1. Filter Profile (Invoices or Payments etc.)
  2. Activation Status
  3. Calculation Formula

I have defined two rules as shown below

As shown below ,the first rule activates the RIB Rule for my FM Area for FM Filter profile 2000 (invoices) and set the calculation Rule Z001 as default which can be overwritten in the next derivation rule step if needed.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Besides the above I have configured below two budget types & assigned them to their respective processes in Funds Management Government >>>BCS >>> Budgeting>>> Definition of Budget Data>>> Define Budget Types

RIBS      RIB Source

RIBT       RIB Target

 

 

 

 

 

Above two Budget types have been assigned to the status for my FM Area in the IMG Path Funds Management Government >>>BCS >>> Budgeting>>> Control Budgeting Flow >>> Edit Status

 

 

 

 

 

Process Flow with Business Case:

We have a business case where sales commission and advertising expenses budget is dependent on the earned revenue by the sales business development team.

We have a fund center Sales Operation 9090 & commitment item 912001 which is assigned to a derivation rule in FMDERIVER to Profit center 9090 and GL 511021 (Revenue). This means that whenever revenue invoice is posted to profit center 9090 and GL Code 511021then Sales Operation Fund center 9090 and Commitment item 912001 will be derived as posting address.

There is another department in Sales which is Sale Business development who gets paid commission, commission expenses payment needs a budget for the commission to be paid over and management wants the payment of commission to be related to the recognition of the revenue their operations brough to the company during the year by executing sales deals.

The fund center for it is 9095. Commission expenses budget is to be recorded on Commitment item 635001.

The Commission Expenses budget will be 6 % of total revenue posted on Sales department.

We have created a calculation formula with the 6% rule of posted revenue with no upper and lower limits meaning we are disregarding the budget posted on Sales operation Fund center.

 

 

 

 

 

Generate RIB Rules:

We will generate RIB object rule master data in t code FMRBGENMD - Generate Master Data for RIB.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

I have posted an accounting document with a value of 1 million Revenue with Value type Invoices, the filter profile of Invoice is assigned to my derivation rule to derive receiving FMAA to increase expense budget. System should propose us an increase of 6%*1,000,000.00= 60,000 SAR

 

 

 

 

 

 

RIB Monitor (RIB Ledger):

RIB Ledger 9M collects all the information relevent to RIB online i-e whenever RIB relevant posting happens then agrregates information automatically. FMRBMON01 is based on the information stored on RIB ledger .We can run FMRBMON01 - Overview of RIB Values to see value of contributor and receiver FMAA.

You can below that for RIB object 9090 the posted amount is updated as 1,000,000 and transferable amount is showing as 60,000 which is as exactly as per the calculation formula.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Simulate/Post Revenue Increasing the Budget:

Now we go to FMMPRBB - Increase Budget from Revenues to calculate and hence post increase in the budget.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Upon execution in the live run, it will post budget document as shown below.

You can see that the system is proposing to post 60,000 EURO budget on the RBBT side which is the received side and hence it has increased the budget on the expense commitment item side.

 

 

 

 

 

 

FMRP_RW_BUDCON Impact:

Budget has been increased on the FMAA as shown below.

 

 

 

 

 

RIB Monitor FMRBMON01 - Overview of RIB Values report also shows transferred amount of 60,000 EURO on the receiver side which concluded our blog 😊

 

 

 

 

 

 

I hope above knowledge article will add value to SAP Community members and our community will continue to thrive. Between it took me 27 days to conclude this article because of busy schedule 😊.

I would also suggest and urge community members to keep sharing their knowledge on this forum as there is a famous saying  "If you have knowledge, let others light their candles in it"

Useful Notes:

  1. You can delete the RIB rules created earlier which are no longer used now using FMRBDEL, the prerequisite is that no RIB document should be created.
  2. You can copy FMRBCPY - Copy program to copy RIB rules from one year to another year.
  3. You must reconstruct index FMRBIDXREC for assigning RIB if there is change in RIB object derivation strategy

entry.title: Re: Fiori & CAP: how can I prefill key field of draft-ena...
entry.content:
@shf

If you annotate the key field with `@readonly`, that field will not appear in the create dialog.

namespace my.bookshop;

entity Books {
  key ID : Integer @readonly @title: 'ID';
  key shop : String @title: 'Shop';
  title  : String @title: 'Title';
  stock  : Integer @title: 'Stock';
}

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Demand and Supply Segmentation – A Solution Validation for Life Sciences
entry.content:
Introduction

Building on my previous blog post, ‘SAP S/4HANA Demand and Supply Segmentation - A Practical Guide for Solution Architects’, I’d like to delve deeper into the solution’s current capabilities and share insights drawn from numerous customer engagements.

Over the past two years as a Business Transformation Consultant, I’ve supported several Life Sciences customers across pre-sales, prototyping, and implementation activities. These experiences have provided valuable lessons that I’m excited to share.

Rather than a step-by-step implementation guide, this post offers practical insights to help with project scoping and planning.

Solution Validation

Customers' interest in Demand and Supply Segmentation remains strong, as its value proposition effectively addresses the key challenges faced by Life Sciences organizations and other regulated industries. However, depending on specific business process requirements and either the current or target SAP S/4HANA release, questions have emerged regarding solution coverage, interfacing, master data harmonization, and change management.

Already in earlier releases, SAP has addressed essential functional gaps in Demand and Supply Segmentation through enhancements in SAP S/4HANA:

  • 2021 Release: Focus on Production Planning for Process Industries (PPPI).
  • 2022 Release: Expanded capabilities in Embedded Warehouse Management (eEWM), Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (ePPDS), and Quality Management (QM).

As part of an extended Proof-of-Concept (PoC) and also an actual implementation for two large Life Sciences customers, we successfully validated a comprehensive end-to-end (E2E) logistical process. This included all major business areas such as Supply Chain Planning and Execution, Procurement, Order-to-Cash, Manufacturing, and Quality Management, utilizing SAP S/4HANA 2023 PCE/OP FPS02.

In summary, Demand and Supply Segmentation now supports a wide range of standard business processes, as well as key processes in the following areas (refer to figure below):

  • Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP)
  • Quality Management (QM)
  • Production Planning for Process Industries (PPPI)
  • Embedded Extended Warehouse Management (eEWM)
  • Embedded Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (ePPDS)

Lessons Learned

Demand and Supply Segmentation has demonstrated its ability to address critical challenges for Life Sciences organizations and other regulated industries. While SAP S/4HANA has progressively closed key functional gaps across multiple releases, practical experience from Proof-of-Concepts (PoCs) and large-scale implementations reveals important considerations for ensuring successful adoption. The following lessons learned highlight key insights and recommendations to guide future projects.

EWM and ePPDS including batch classification and shelf-life planning

  • Add segmentation-specific characteristic to generic EWM batch class SAP_EWM_LOBM (refer to Master Data for Segmentation | SAP Help Portal)
  • Add the same characteristic to batch classes used in relevant material master
  • Don’t use identical names for Segmentation characteristic, strategy and batch class. Reason: with the integration to ePPDS, automatically a Segmentation characteristic is created with the name of the strategy (refer to Characteristic-Dependent Planning (CDP) | SAP Help Portal).
  • For ePPDS integration, both Routing (ROU) and Bill of Materials (BOM) were initially required for Segmentation relevance. However, SAP Note 3420855 - PPDS: Routing relevancy is mandatory at the structure level to activated segmentation with ppds - SAP for Me has relaxed this requirement. Depending on your scenario, flagging either ROU or BOM may now be sufficient.

Segmentation-dependent MRP data

  • In S/4HANA, segmentation-dependent MRP data is no longer available (refer to SAP Note 3296784 - S4TWL - Segmentation Simplification - SAP for Me). In order to allow, for instance, segmentation-dependent lot-size data a custom-table similar to SGT_MRP or EINSGT needs to be created and used during source of supply selection. In ePPDS this could be implemented using the BAdIs /SAPAPO/RRP_PLANNING, /SAPAPO/RRP_SRC_EXIT and /SAPAPO/RRP_LOTSIZE_STD.

Goods Receipt in EWM

  • During the Goods Receipt (GR) process for Inbound Deliveries (IBDs) in Embedded Warehouse Management (EWM), we observed a segmentation-specific behavior: if stock already exists for a material and batch, the new GR is always posted to the existing batch, and no new batch is created. This behavior can be adjusted using the BAdIs ES_BADI_SGT_DET_VALIDATE or BADI_BATCH_DET, specifically the method MODIFY_BATCH.

Master Data Governance

  • Currently, Master Data Governance (MDG) does not support the maintenance of segmentation fields in the material master. To enable this functionality, the screens and services must be enhanced as part of a project-specific enhancement.

Audit Trail

  • By default, database tables for Demand and Supply Segmentation configuration don't have change history activated. However, table logging can be enabled easily using the Audit Trail Tool (transaction S_AUT01) for the following tables:

    • SGT_CATS: Segmentation Structure Fields Table
    • SGT_CSGR_L: Segmentation Structures - Short Texts
    • SGT_CSGR_T: Segmentation Structures
    • SGT_COVS_L: Segmentation Strategies - Short Texts
    • SGT_COVS_T: Segmentation Strategies

Interfacing through IDOCs, BAPIs, APIs, etc.

  • Certain IDocs, such as MATMAS and BATMAS, already include the Segment field. However, others, like LOIPRO, can be enhanced using the BAdI CO_MES_INT_IDOC.
  • Similarly, some APIs, such as API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV and API_PROCESS_ORDER_2_SRV, support the segment field natively. In contrast, APIs like CO_MDM_PRD_BULK_REPL_REQ_OUT require enhancements to include this functionality.
  • Additionally, the segment structure and strategy can be interfaced through the following APIs:
    • API_SEGMENTATIONSTRUCTURE
    • API_SEGMENT_SRV
    • API_DFLTSEGMTNMAINTENANCE

Migration and post-Go-Live Segmentation activation/deactivation

  • A frequently discussed topic is whether Segmentation can be activated or deactivated for materials post-Go-Live, particularly when existing inventory or open documents, such as sales orders, purchase orders, or process orders, are involved.

  • Currently, there is no standard solution for this scenario, except for leveraging the "Enable Blank" approach within the segmentation structure.

  • However, this situation bears similarities to the process of batch management activation and deactivation for materials. With the tools provided by the Batch Management Team (refer to SAP Note 533276), it is theoretically possible to apply a similar approach to Segmentation activation or deactivation.

    Note: This concept remains untested and requires thorough prototyping and validation. Additionally, the Batch Management Tool must be purchased and customized on a project-specific basis.

Future Solution Capabilities

SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP)

  • The introduction of Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP) in SAP IBP (Release 2411) opens exciting possibilities for integrating Demand and Supply Segmentation with advanced planning processes (refer to Setting Up Characteristics-Based Planning | SAP Help Portal).
  • This feature is currently available by request, offering early adopters a competitive advantage.

Summary

Demand and Supply Segmentation has proven to be a vital solution for overcoming key challenges faced by Life Sciences and other regulated industries, with SAP continuously enhancing its functionality to meet evolving needs. The insights and lessons gathered from real-world implementations not only highlight the maturity of the solution but also provide valuable guidance for future projects.

Looking ahead, the integration of Segmentation with advanced planning tools like SAP IBP’s Characteristics-Based Planning represents a promising step towards even greater process optimization and strategic flexibility for businesses.

entry.title: How to terminate an employee in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We're currently using an SAP on-premise system, and in it, a lot of Personnel Administration (PA) functionalities. We're planning to move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition though, and with the migration, we have a doubt.

In the on-premise system, we have the termination action to terminate an employment and update the employment status to withdrawn or inactive. We would like to know then:

  • What's the process to terminate (not delete) an employee and have its status set to inactive in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Thanks,
Implementation experts


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to terminate an employee in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'd like to quickly link one of our official documentation pages, below:

  • Manage Workforce > How to Create and Correct Work Agreements | SAP Help Portal

In it, it's stated that:

  • You can't delete an existing work agreement, you can only terminate a work agreement by changing the end date.

This means that, to terminate an work agreement, you'd simply need to change its end date in the Manage Workforce Fiori application. Additionally, how to do this is also explained in the same page:

  • If you need to correct the End Date or the Is Contingent Worker of fields for the initial employment, choose Edit and change the date directly on the UI.

I hope this helps with your query!

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Unlock Efficient Resource Management with AI in SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Introduction:

Resource management in projects presents a significant challenge for both project and resource managers, particularly when it comes to aligning project resource demands with the appropriate employees. The dynamic nature of projects, coupled with the varied skills and availability of team members, makes manual staffing processes increasingly complex and inefficient. Managers must not only consider the current workload and expertise of their employees but also anticipate future project needs, which can lead to bottlenecks, underutilization of resources, or project delays. The need for IT solutions that can streamline and optimize these processes has become paramount to ensure that projects are successfully executed and delivered on time and within budget. This is where SAP's new solution, SAP Project and Resource Management, steps in, offering a comprehensive approach to enhance the staffing process, thereby enabling companies to manage their resources more effectively and efficiently. 

SPRM (former SAP Cloud for Projects) is a standalone cloud solution built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) that facilitates the management of resources for project stakeholders. It can be utilized as an advanced resource management tool for ETO (Engineer-to-Order) and / or PSA (Professional Service Automation) projects. Here SPRM provides standard integration with enterprise and commercial projects managed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition Due to its modular design and the availability of APIs it is possible to connect SPRM easily with other ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA cloud, private edition or other third-party solutions. 

You can find a brief introduction and learn more about the solution in the following link. In this blog, we will focus more on the business challenges in resource management and how SAP Business AI can significantly optimize the staffing process. 

 

Skills Challenges:

As a resource manager, finding skilled individuals for a project can be time-consuming. New skills are frequently required, but employee profiles often lack up-to-date skill information. Besides this, project managers must have a solid understanding of available skill sets and manually interact with candidates to find the right fit. Keeping accurate employee skill data is challenging due to evolving technologies and methodologies. Demand for specific skills can be complex, leading to few qualified candidates. Finding alternatives requires understanding employee profiles and the business context, which is time-consuming. Additionally, maintaining new skills and certifications adds further complexity. However, organizations that effectively manage resource demand and supply can quickly respond to market changes and customer needs. This gives them a competitive edge and boosts productivity. 

 

Intelligent Skills Matching using Artificial Intelligence 

The SAP Business AI-driven “Advanced Skill Match” feature in SAP Project and Resource Management helps resource managers quickly identify suitable candidates for project roles. This tool enhances the search process with a similarity analysis, providing a list of potential candidates who not only directly match the required skills but also those with similar skill sets. 

This feature is particularly useful when no direct skill match is found. By suggesting candidates with similar skills, it saves resource managers from lengthy searches, numerous phone calls, and reading multiple profiles and CVs. As a result, it significantly speeds up the process of finding the right resources for projects, accelerating both project setup and execution." 

Overview of the advanced skill matching functionality 

In the “Staff Resource Requests” App, the system suggests candidates who closely match the requested skills, in addition to those who match exactly based on the required skills and those of the resource. For instance, Anuj Gupta has a 0% match for the demanded skills “Agile Coach” and “SAP Cloud ERP” since the current skills maintained in his profile are “SAP S/4HANA Cloud” and “Scrum Master”. Despite this, since he is 100% available, the system generates a 50% total match as this is the average of the availability match percentage and the skills match percentage. 

Moreover, the AI-powered “Advanced Skill Matching” feature recognizes the similarity between “Agile Coaching” and “Scrum Master” skills. It gives Anuj a 3-star match and suggests him as another potential candidate with similar skills. 

In summary, intelligent/advanced skills matching based on similar skills can enhance project staffing by improving efficiency, resource utilization, cost-effectiveness, and the overall success of the project. It also promotes a more agile, collaborative, and well-developed workforce, ultimately contributing to better project outcomes and more effective talent management strategies. The intelligent skills matching AI functionality is delivered in release “2412” with controlled availability as the SAP AI terms of agreement needs to be signed before using the AI driven intelligent skill matching capabilities.  You can find more information about the activation process here.

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item [DP92]
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how Credit Management functions works in dynamic item processing.

The credit management function adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Dynamic Item Processor Framework (DIP) [transaction DP92], During this process a Debit Memo Request (DMR) document is generated. The DMR that is generated out of DIP could qualify for credit commitment update. If the predecessor transaction Service Order is Credit relevant, Hence Credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place.Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”.

Type of Credit Exposure Category

Name of Credit Exposure Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance types in service order for which credit management gets triggered. 

In this blog we will focusing more on Summarized Billing and Itemized Billing Processes.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is enabled for credit check.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enable.

Check the DIP Profile Setting: Transaction “ODP1” .

Summarized Billing Relevance with DCD Case:

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as “Summarized Billing” and enter the DIP Profile.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item. If the credit check is not successful, then service order will go into DCD case (Documented Credit Decisions). In this case user must go via approval process to get rid of the DCD blocker. Only authorized person can approve the DCD case for blocked service order depends upon the business.

Go to the “Manage Documented Credit decisions” app.

Enter the service order and click on release to approve the service order. Once the service order is approved then item will set it in “RELEASED” status.

Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit commitments exposure in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract) category.   

Check the credit commitment exposure in 110 category on service order released.

 

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item.

Manual release of the PMO in transaction via “IW32”.

 

Posting PMO Confirmations via IW48 transaction to create the Debit Memo Request. Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry. Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Click on Create Billing Request.

Debit Memo Request (DMR) is Created.

 

Check the credit commitment exposures. It should get updated in 410 category on DMR creation.

 

Create the Debit Memo.

 

As user created the debit memo, Hence credit commitment exposures will move from 410 to 500 category.

Check the 410 Commitment. As the 410 exposures moved to 500 category on successful creation of debit memo.

No value should persist in the 410 category.

Post the debit memo for accounting. Go to VF02 and Click on Release to Accounting flag.

Check the credit commitment exposures in 200 category

This is how credit exposures process work when there is DCD case.

Next we will learn about Itemized Billing.

Itemized Billing Relevance:

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item.

Manual release of the PMO in transaction IW32.

Posting PMO Confirmations in transaction IW48 to create the Debit Memo Request.

Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry.

Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Credit commitment exposures will get updated in 410 category on DMR creation.

Here credit commitments get calculated based on net value and taxes.

Create the Invoice after DMR creation.(Debit Memo)

Updating the 500 category upon successful creation of the invoice.

Removing the credit commitment exposures from 410 category.

Release the Invoice (Debit Memo) to accounting to move the credit commitments exposure to 200 category.

 

The distinct feature in this process flow is explained in detail how the credit management work in advanced execution order item in Service Order.

entry.title: Automate Job Requisition Updates from Position Data Using Integration Center
entry.content:
Introduction

In the dynamic world of recruitment, keeping job requisitions in sync with position updates is crucial. Often, organizations face the challenge of manually updating job requisitions when there are changes in position fields such as band, designation, location, cost center, or department. This blog post will guide you through automating this process using SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center.

Pre-requisites

  1. Define Std_position_obj in the Recruiting requisition template.
  2. Populate position details in the requisition.
  3. Create Standard position object field and assign appropriate permissions.
  4. Integration Center 
  • Create Std_position_obj in Recruiting Job requisition template and to have position details populated in the requisition.

Note: Multiselect should be False, solution will not work in case multiselect = true.

  • Assign Read/Write permission as per business requirement (I assign Read permission in Pre-approve and Approve Job Requisition Status)

Scenario

Consider a scenario where fields like Business Unit, Department, Cost Center, Location, and Job Level need to be updated in the job requisition. This automation ensures that any changes in these fields within the position are reflected in the job requisition.

My Existing Position When requisition is created

My Job Requisition when it was created

 

  • Integration Center Job to Update the Job Requisition Field

Steps to Set Up Integration

1. Navigate to Integration Center

  • Go to Admin Center >> Integration Center.
  • Select My Integrations >> Create >> More Integration Type.

2. Configure Integration

  • Choose Trigger Type as Scheduled.
  • Set Source and Destination as SuccessFactors.
  • Format as OData V2.

3. Select Entities

  • Source Entity: JobReqGOPosition.
  • Destination Entity: Job Requisition.

4. Map Fields

Map fields from the source to the destination as per business requirements. Example mappings include:

  • Job Requisition Id (jobReqId) from JobRequisition <<-- Job Req Id (jobReqId) from JobReqGOPosition.
  • Department: department_obj/externalCode in FODepartment <<-- Code (externalCode) from Department (FODepartment).
